run_summary: Portfolio is +2.4% overall with 5 open positions. AVAV has deteriorated sharply on restatement/officer departure (4.02+5.02) filed 6/22, now sitting -7.64% after 1 day. NTLA, PTCT, and ALK remain solidly positive on their original catalyst theses (NTLA +25%, PTCT +12%, ALK +6%). ALGN is flat but facing a 5.02/7.01 disclosure today. Among new flags: AYI re-flags at conf 3 (re-entry bar: conf>=4 + new info) — recent close was +2.77% on identical thesis, so WATCH. AVAV is already in portfolio (flag is confirmatory on held position). KTOS and CALX are marginal OVERDONE/UNDERDONE conf 3 setups with weak catalysts — both SKIP. ALGN in new flags is the same name we hold; that flag is OVERDONE conf 3 and argues for a small ADD if thesis strengthening, but it's not.

entries.name: Acuity Inc.
entries.catalyst: Q4 earnings release filed via 8-K item 2.02
entries.thesis: A sharp single-day gap on an earnings 8-K with no offsetting negative items (no 5.02, no 4.02, no impairment) suggests a clean beat-and-raise. Magnitude is large but coincides with XLI ripping and an electrification/lighting demand narrative. Without seeing guidance specifics, the move looks proportionate to a meaningful beat rather than excessive — fading purely on size would be fighting both the print and sector tape.
entries.what_kills: If the beat was driven by one-time items, M&A contribution from the Arrow acquisition masking organic softness, or if guidance was merely reiterated rather than raised — then the gap is overextended and faders have a setup.
entries.reasoning: AYI is a re-entry name: you closed it 8 days ago for a +2.77% gain on OVERDONE conf 3 thesis (sympathy with industrials on CPI/Iran). You exited because the thesis direction REVERSED (stock went -10.37% in 5 days instead of fading as OVERDONE thesis predicted). Today's new flag re-flags AYI on a RATIONAL conf 3 basis (clean 2.02 8-K, beat-and-raise, XLI ripping, electrification narrative). This is nominally DIFFERENT from the prior exit thesis (which was pure sympathy fade) — the new flag is earnings-driven, not sector-sympathy-driven. However, the re-entry bar requires BOTH (1) confidence >= 4 AND (2) genuinely new information. The new flag is conf 3, which means the execution layer will downgrade BUY to WATCH regardless. Additionally, the 'new information' (earnings beat) does not invalidate the prior exit reasoning (direction reversed). The name is interesting, but confidence and discipline require WATCH here. If AYI stabilizes and re-confirms the beat thesis over the next 2-3 days, or if you see a new catalyst (guidance raise, analyst initiations), re-evaluate at that point.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing non-reliance on previously issued financials (4.02) plus officer departure (5.02); earnings scheduled 2026-06-29 after hours
entries.thesis: A 4.02 non-reliance filing is among the most severe 8-K codes — it tells investors prior financials cannot be trusted. Pairing it with a 5.02 officer departure and an earnings print 5 days out compounds the risk: the restatement scope is unknown, the audit/legal overhang is just beginning, and the earnings call now becomes a damage-control event rather than a normal print. A move of this magnitude looks insufficient for that combination; restatement names typically grind lower for weeks as analysts withdraw estimates and the scope expands.
entries.what_kills: If the restatement is narrowly scoped to a single non-cash item with no period-over-period EPS impact, and the departed officer is a planned transition unrelated to the restatement, the overhang dissipates quickly. A clean reaffirmation of guidance on the earnings call would also kill the thesis.
entries.reasoning: AVAV is already in the portfolio (1 day old, -7.64% unrealized). The new flag re-surfaces it as UNDERDONE conf 4 with the same 4.02+5.02+earnings catalyst. This is NOT a new flag; it is a confirmation of the position already held. Addressed in position_decisions above: the position is being EXITED due to the entry conviction being insufficient given the -7.64% 1-day move and the opacity around restatement scope. The re-flag does not change that decision.

entries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
entries.catalyst: Iran de-escalation narrative (Energy Sec: Hormuz reopened, 72 ships through in 24h) pressuring small-cap defense beta names
entries.thesis: KTOS, AVAV, and MRCY all down meaningfully on a day when the broader defense ETF (ITA) is flat — suggests these specific small-cap defense plays are being de-rated on the Strait of Hormuz reopening / Iran ceasefire narrative. KTOS has now given back ~15% in five days. With ITA only flat (not down), the small-cap defense names appear to be overshooting on de-escalation while large primes hold up — that asymmetry usually mean-reverts when the next geopolitical headline hits. Lean bearish-continuation-fade i.e. underdone on the downside thesis is weak; more likely an overdone-down setup. Reclassifying: the move looks OVERDONE relative to ITA.
entries.what_kills: Iran-Israel ceasefire holding for multiple weeks with no flare-ups; DoD budget headlines targeting unmanned/tactical programs specifically
entries.reasoning: KTOS flagged as UNDERDONE conf 3 on small-cap defense de-rating due to Iran de-escalation. The flag's own prose self-contradicts: it opens 'UNDERDONE on the downside thesis is weak; more likely an overdone-down setup. Reclassifying: the move looks OVERDONE.' The logic is that small-cap defense names (KTOS, AVAV, MRCY) are down ~15% while ITA (sector ETF) is flat, suggesting mean-reversion setup. However: (1) no catalyst URL is provided; (2) the 'catalyst' is a narrative (Iran de-escalation) with no SEC filing or time-specific event; (3) the flag is internally inconsistent (UNDERDONE vs. OVERDONE); (4) confidence 3 with no clear entry setup. The peer-dispersion fade angle is real but not actionable without better definition of entry conditions (what is ITA at, what is the reversion target?). Skipping — the setup is too vague and the thesis lacks precision.

entries.name: Calix, Inc
entries.catalyst: Unknown — elevated volume without visible news
entries.thesis: CALX -3% today on 2.66x average volume — the volume spike is the interesting signal, not the price move. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no obvious peer drag (broadband-access equipment isn't part of the SMH wreckage). High-volume selling in a $2.3B name without identifiable catalyst often reflects fund-level repositioning that reverses when the seller is done. Tech-sector beta weak today provides cover but doesn't fully explain. Modest fade setup if next session shows volume normalizing.
entries.what_kills: BEAD/rural broadband funding cut announcement or major tier-1 telco capex revision — this would justify and extend the move
entries.reasoning: CALX flagged OVERDONE conf 3 on -3% price move with 2.66x volume spike, no visible catalyst. The thesis is that high-volume selling without news often reflects fund repositioning that reverses. Risks: (1) confidence 3 is marginal for any entry; (2) no catalyst_url; (3) the 'what_kills' scenario (BEAD/rural broadband funding cuts) is a real operational risk that is material enough to require pre-entry conviction this setup doesn't have; (4) the move is small (-3%) and the volume spike could reflect legitimate repositioning OR the start of a multi-day grind lower if a cut is coming. The fade setup is not compelling enough to justify entry at conf 3 with no catalyst specificity. Skipping.

entries.name: Align Technology, Inc.
entries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22 with Items 5.02 (officer change) and 7.01 (Reg FD)
entries.thesis: ALGN dropping ~4.5% on a 5.02 + 7.01 combo while XLV is up 0.44% and XBI up 3.65% suggests the market is reading the departure as material. But 5.02 without an accompanying 2.02 (earnings) or 4.02 (restatement) is usually a single-event story — and ALGN is a quality compounder that has historically been a fade-the-overreaction name. If this is a planned CEO/CFO succession with named successor (which the parallel 7.01 Reg FD often signals — companies pair them when they want to control narrative), the drop is likely overdone in a tape with healthcare bid.
entries.what_kills: Departure is the CFO mid-quarter with no successor named, OR the Reg FD disclosure contains a guidance cut — either makes the drop justified or even insufficient
entries.reasoning: ALGN flagged as OVERDONE conf 3 based on 5.02+7.01 (officer departure + Reg FD) on a -4.57% move in a positive healthcare tape. The flag argues the move is overdone because 5.02 without 2.02/4.02 is usually a single-event story and ALGN is a quality compounder that fades overreactions. However, you already own ALGN (6 days, -0.72%, position size 0.35% equity). The position_decisions above assess thesis_status as 'weakening' (officer departure is new execution risk). A fresh 'ADD' decision would require thesis to be strengthening, not weakening. The flag is interesting from a fade angle but not a conviction to add. Skipping on the new flag; the existing position remains HOLD per position_decisions logic above.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.thesis: ONTO is moving with SMH +2.9% and broad semi-cap equipment strength (VECO, AEIS also up 4-6%). No company-specific 8-K. The move is a sector beta event, not idiosyncratic, and magnitude is roughly in line with peers given ONTO's higher beta to advanced packaging/HBM demand narrative.
entries.what_kills: Sector reversal; ONTO underperforming peers on a flat semi tape would suggest there was company-specific bid that won't repeat.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
entries.thesis: AEIS gets both the XLI +2.17% bid and the SMH +2.9% bid given its semiconductor power conversion exposure. No 8-K, no earnings — just a name that screens well on a day when both its tapes are green. Five-day +6% suggests momentum already building, not an isolated gap.
entries.what_kills: A specific semi-cap negative datapoint or industrial reversal would unwind quickly given the move is purely sector-driven.

entries.name: Repligen Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — possibly bioprocessing sector rotation or conference presentation
entries.thesis: RGEN is up with XLV +1.49% and XBI +1.26% but the relative move is larger than sector beta would predict, and five-day +8.75% suggests something more than tape. No 8-K, no earnings catalyst, no news hit. Could be conference presentation, analyst day, or M&A whisper — but without evidence I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: If purely sector beta, the relative outperformance fades on any healthcare reversal.

entries.name: A.O. Smith Corporation
entries.catalyst: 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing officer/director departure or appointment
entries.thesis: A 5.02 officer change can cut either direction — planned retirement vs forced exit vs new strategic hire. The stock is rallying meaningfully on a day when industrials broadly are strong (XLI +1.2%), so part of the move is sector beta. Without reading the 8-K exhibit to determine whether this is a CEO succession, an activist-driven replacement, or a routine board change, the directional read isn't articulable at conf 3+.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Housing-policy news flow (Trump housing bill discussion, Save America Act); macro risk-on in cyclicals
entries.thesis: BLDR's double-digit move aligns with the Trump housing-bill / Save America Act news cycle (housing affordability legislation discussion, possible interest rate pressure narrative) and broad industrial/small-cap risk-on (XLI +1.2%, IWM +0.5%). No 8-K, no earnings catalyst — this looks like macro positioning into a housing-policy event combined with sector rotation. Magnitude is large but matches the macro setup; no clear over/underreaction edge.
entries.what_kills: Single-stock unwind tomorrow with no follow-through from peers would suggest a squeeze rather than thesis, but doesn't constitute a directional fade signal on its own

entries.name: JetBlue Airways Corporation
entries.catalyst: Iran de-escalation / Strait of Hormuz reopening pressing crude lower; airline fuel-cost tailwind
entries.thesis: JBLU and ALK both rallying on the same day XLE is down -1.6% and the Energy Sec confirms Hormuz traffic restored. Airlines are the cleanest beneficiary of lower jet-fuel input cost expectations. The move is well-justified by the catalyst chain; no obvious mispricing edge in either direction.
entries.what_kills: Hormuz traffic disruption resuming, OPEC+ cut announcement

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: 6/18 Reg FD 8-K of unclear content; ongoing met-coal commodity weakness
entries.thesis: AMR down nearly 5% today and ~12% over 5 days, with a 7.01 Reg FD disclosure filed last week. Reg FD items are catch-all — could be investor presentation, could be guidance update — and without the filing contents we cannot tell which. Coking coal pricing is also pressured by global steel demand uncertainty. Cannot construct a 3+ confidence call in either direction without reading the 6/18 8-K exhibit.
entries.what_kills: Filing turns out to be benign (conference presentation) — then today's selling is pure commodity tape and likely RATIONAL given met-coal spot weakness

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics
entries.catalyst: Sector rotation into biotech; possible takeout/data speculation not in 8-K stream
entries.thesis: BEAM up on the day in line with a very strong XBI tape, extending a notable multi-day run. No catalyst_signals means the move is riding the sector bid rather than a discrete event. Magnitude is reasonable given XBI's strength; nothing to fade or chase without identifying the specific driver of the multi-day run.
entries.what_kills: XBI rolls over; BEAM gives back gains faster than the sector

entries.name: Pool Corporation
entries.catalyst: Unknown — no 8-K, no news, no earnings proximity flagged
entries.thesis: POOL trading at nearly 7x average volume on a modest price move is unusual — volume disconnect from price typically signals institutional repositioning, index rebalance, or a block trade rather than fundamental news. No 8-K, no earnings window flagged. Cannot articulate directional thesis without knowing the source of flow.
entries.what_kills: Volume turns out to be a single block cross with no follow-through tomorrow

entries.name: Watsco, Inc.
entries.thesis: WSO up alongside BLDR with no company news suggests sympathy buying on a building products day. Magnitude is in line with the risk-on tape and small/mid industrial bid (XLI +0.73%, IWM +2%). No mispricing call — just observe that the housing-adjacent distributors are trading together.
entries.what_kills: WSO-specific 8-K appears showing M&A or guide cut/raise

entries.name: Exelixis, Inc.
entries.catalyst: USTR tariff probe on Germany drug pricing dragging pharma sector
entries.thesis: EXEL trading heavy on elevated volume against an up tape, in line with XLV -0.87% weakness. The US tariff probe on German drug pricing is a pharma policy headline that drags pharma broadly; EXEL is US-focused oncology so the read-through is indirect. Slight overdone bias — pricing policy fears tend to mean-revert in single-drug oncology names without direct EU exposure — but conviction low without firm-specific clarity.
entries.what_kills: An EXEL-specific negative readout, FDA action, or competitive trial result emerges — would justify the weakness fundamentally
